name: Easy Dataset
tagline: Turn your documents into high-quality fine-tuning datasets for LLMs
seoTitle: Easy Dataset: Build LLM Fine-Tuning Datasets from Docs
metaDescription: Easy Dataset is an open-source tool that converts PDF, Markdown, DOCX and other documents into structured QA datasets for LLM fine-tuning, RAG, and evaluation.
overview: Easy Dataset is an open-source application built for creating fine-tuning datasets for large language models. It gives you an intuitive interface plus built-in document parsing, smart text splitting, and data cleaning, so you can take domain documents and turn them into high-quality structured data.

You feed it files in formats like PDF, Markdown, DOCX, TXT, or EPUB. The tool splits the text into segments, generates relevant questions, and uses an LLM API to write answers. The result is a clean dataset you can use for model fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), or model evaluation.

It works with any LLM API that follows the OpenAI format, including OpenAI, Ollama for local models, Zhipu AI, OpenRouter, and others. Finished datasets export in common formats such as Alpaca and ShareGPT, and can be sent straight to a LLaMA Factory config or uploaded to the Hugging Face Hub.
features: Smart document processing for PDF, Markdown, DOCX, TXT, and EPUB, with several text-splitting algorithms (Markdown structure, recursive separators, fixed length, code-aware)
Automatic question generation from text segments, plus an LLM-powered answer generation step that can include Chain of Thought reasoning
Multiple dataset types: single-turn QA, multi-turn dialogue, image QA, and data distillation directly from domain topics without uploading documents
Built-in model evaluation system with automated judge-model scoring and a human blind-test arena for comparing two models' answers
Export to Alpaca, ShareGPT, and Multilingual-Thinking formats as JSON or JSONL, with one-click LLaMA Factory config generation and direct Hugging Face upload
Wide model compatibility with any OpenAI-format API, including OpenAI, Ollama, Zhipu AI, Alibaba Bailian, and OpenRouter

gettingStarted.intro: You can run Easy Dataset from source with Node.js, use the official Docker image, or download a desktop client for Windows, macOS, or Linux. The steps below cover the NPM and Docker paths from the project README.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Download the source code and move into the project folder.

steps.heading: Install dependencies and start the app
steps.body: Install the Node packages, build the project, then start the server.
steps.code:
npm install
npm run build
npm run start

steps.heading: Or run with Docker
steps.body: From the cloned repository, start the official image with docker-compose. The database file is initialized automatically on first startup.

steps.heading: Open the app
steps.body: Visit the local address in your browser, then connect your LLM API to begin generating datasets.

useCases: Convert internal manuals, research papers, or knowledge bases into single-turn or multi-turn QA datasets for fine-tuning a domain-specific model
Generate evaluation datasets (true/false, single-choice, short-answer, open-ended) and score model answers automatically or through a human blind test
Prepare data for RAG pipelines by cleaning and structuring source documents into question-answer pairs
Build image QA datasets from a folder of images or a PDF for training and testing vision models
Export ready-to-use datasets to LLaMA Factory or upload them directly to the Hugging Face Hub
